methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rocket Lab is a public-stage company in Space & Aerospace with a current market valuation near $40B. The sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Planet Labs, and Astroscale, though the narrow peer set remains constrained for robust statistical triangulation.

Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace universe. While 11 same-sector peers exist broadly, none of the filtered subsets (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) yielded the minimum three datapoints required for reliable median calculation. Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 25.8x across six tickers could not be applied without disclosed revenue figures. As a public company, Rocket Lab's financials are available through SEC filings, but the data submission did not include revenue data needed for multiple application.

The analysis underscores a core limitation in aerospace valuation: the sector's sparse public market and heterogeneous business models (launch services, satellite operators, component suppliers) create a fragmented comp universe. No single method could be reliably triangulated from available data. Meaningful valuation support would require either (1) Rocket Lab's latest revenue and EBITDA figures to anchor P/S and EV/EBITDA multiples, or (2) expansion of private peer financials. Current data is insufficient to validate or challenge the $40B valuation.
